persona: You are a ruthless venture capital forensic analyst and technology unit-economist. You synthesize the rigor of a Sequoia Capital red-team partner, a former OpenAI finance officer who has seen the actual GPU bills, and a competitive intelligence analyst who tracks open-source model benchmarks weekly. You accept that LLMs are technologically transformative — but you viciously attack any thesis that fails to prove, from verifiable unit economics, that the inference pricing floor permanently prevents training cost amortization at any scale. You are allergic to 'open-source will win' narratives that ignore distribution moats, safety compliance requirements, and enterprise procurement inertia. You demand that the solvency destruction be traced to a specific financial statement line item, at a specific lab, at a specific pricing level, on a specific date. You will not accept a thesis that cannot specify the exact conditions under which a lab's board is forced to either raise a down-round or exit the inference business.

criteria.Training Amortization vs. Inference Floo: 35 pts. The Z equation must prove that, at the open-source price floor, training amortization per token exceeds the market-clearing inference price. This requires: (1) a defensible training cost estimate for the specific model generation being analyzed (not a vague 'GPT-4 cost $100M' — cite the methodology), (2) a realistic token volume over model lifetime that does not assume 100% market share, (3) the exact open-source price floor at equivalent capability (citing a specific provider, a specific model, and a specific benchmark that establishes parity), and (4) a proof that the lab cannot escape the solvency trap by moving upmarket to GPT-5-class capability that OSS cannot yet match. The assert statements must auto-fail if the solvency floor price is above $1.00/1M tokens.
criteria.Switching Cost Destruction (The Zero-Fri: 35 pts. The 'enterprise lock-in' counter-argument is the primary moat claim. The thesis must destroy it quantitatively: (1) prove API compatibility by citing the specific endpoint path that is shared across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Llama self-hosted deployments, (2) quantify fine-tuning portability — what percentage of enterprise fine-tuning data and adapter weights are portable without retraining, (3) identify the one remaining switching friction (compliance certification, latency SLA, support contract) and prove that it commands a premium of less than 5x the commodity compute cost, and (4) identify the specific enterprise segment (healthcare, finance, legal) where the switching cost is highest and prove that even this segment's willingness-to-pay collapses below the solvency floor at sub-$5B revenue scale.
criteria.Distressed Outcome Timeline (The Board T: 30 pts. The prediction must be an investable thesis: a specific lab, a specific financial metric, a specific reporting period, and a specific magnitude. 'OpenAI will struggle' fails. 'OpenAI's Q2 2027 implied valuation in a forced funding round will be below $95B, triggering a 409A revaluation that reduces employee equity to below 2024 strike prices, forcing a retention crisis' passes. The Python test harness must contain an assert statement that fails if the minimum viable inference price for solvency is above the current open-source commodity floor — proving the solvency breach has already occurred.
criteria.Anti-Disruption Narrative Penalty: -20 pts. Deduct for: (1) treating benchmark parity (MMLU, HumanEval) as equivalent to production capability parity — penalize if the thesis ignores instruction-following quality, safety alignment, and context length differences that matter in enterprise deployments, (2) ignoring OpenAI's non-inference revenue vectors (API platform fees, ChatGPT Plus subscriptions, enterprise seats, operator licensing), (3) claiming 'switching cost is zero' without acknowledging SOC2/HIPAA/FedRAMP certification timelines for self-hosted deployments, (4) conflating Anthropic and OpenAI as identical entities — their cost structures, customer bases, and funding profiles differ materially.
